steps.title: Calculate the slope of h
steps.content: To find the equation of a linear function, start by calculating its slope. Using the given points h(28) = 15 and h(26) = 22, apply the formula for the slope $m$ between two points $(x_1,y_1)$ and $(x_2, y_2):$ $$m=\frac{y_2-y_1}{x_2-x_1}=\frac{22-15}{26-28}=-\frac{7}{2}.$$

steps.title: Identify the correct equation using the slope and a point
steps.content: With the slope $m=-\frac{7}{2}$ determined, substitute either given point into each option to determine which one matches. Using h(28) = 15, test option B: $$h(x)=-\frac{2}{7}x+23$$ and D: $$h(x)=-\frac{7}{2}x+113.$$ For option B, substituting $x=28$ gives $h(28)= -\frac{2}{7}(28)+23 = 15$, but the slope does not match our calculated value of $-\frac{7}{2}$. Thus, it is incorrect. Testing D with x=28: $$h(28)=-\frac{7}{2}(28)+113=-98+113=15.$$ This matches both the point and our slope.

steps.title: Confirm D as correct
steps.content: That matches choice D — D is correct.
